summary.intent: Port the Nostr DM channel from the openclaw TypeScript project to Rust using nostr-sdk v0.44, achieving feature parity with existing Moltis channels (Telegram, Discord, Slack, MS Teams, WhatsApp, Matrix).
summary.outcome: Fully implemented the moltis-nostr crate (~2,200 LOC across 25 files) with plugin lifecycle, NIP-04/NIP-44 encryption, relay subscription loop, access control, OTP flow, metrics instrumentation, web UI add/edit/onboarding flows, Playwright E2E tests, documentation, and 5 real integration tests against live Nostr relays.

repo: ChannelType enum is #[non_exhaustive] so new variants don't break wildcard match arms in gateway/chat crates
nostr-sdk v0.44 requires explicit feature flags: 'nip04' and 'nip44' are not enabled by default
nostr_sdk::prelude::* imports both nip04 and nip44 with glob re-exports, causing 'unnecessary qualification' lint conflicts — use nostr_sdk::nips::nip04::decrypt directly
All channel configs flow through all_channel_configs() which iterates both named struct fields and the #[serde(flatten)] extra map — matrix was going through extra, not a named field
The moltis_channels contract test suite targets TestPlugin, not real plugins — real plugins need valid credentials to start so they can't be tested with empty config
broadcast::Receiver from client.notifications() must be created BEFORE connect()+subscribe() to avoid missing events
nostr-sdk RelayPoolNotification::Event deduplicates (sent only first time seen); use Message variant for raw relay messages in tests
biome formatter (used for JS) requires chained method calls (.split().map().filter()) on separate lines when line exceeds print width
The website/index.*.html locale files already had Nostr references — only the SVG icon file was missing
Cargo optional dep feature activation: use 'dep?/feature' syntax to activate a feature on an optional dep only when that dep is already enabled, preventing accidental dep pull-in

code.finding: UTF-8 safe truncation: use plaintext.floor_char_boundary(MAX_MESSAGE_BYTES) instead of direct slice — direct slice panics on non-char-boundary
AccountState.config should be Arc<RwLock<NostrAccountConfig>> shared between state and bus loop — cloning config at start time disconnects the bus from live config updates
update_account_config must detect [REDACTED] sentinel in secret_key field and preserve existing key — otherwise a web UI save corrupts the stored key
Collapse nested match+if-let using Rust 2021 match guard syntax: RelayPoolNotification::Message { message: RelayMessage::Event { event, .. }, .. } => if condition { }
Clippy len_without_is_empty: any struct with a len() method must also implement is_empty()
ALL constant and variant count test must be updated when adding new ChannelType variants — there's an explicit assert_eq!(ChannelType::ALL.len(), N) test
client.notifications() creates a temporary that is dropped immediately if not bound — must bind to a variable before the loop

workflow: nostr-sdk Client::new() signature, subscribe() taking a single Filter (not Vec), disconnect() returning () not Result, and to_bech32() returning Result<String, Infallible> all differ from naive assumptions — always check actual crate API before writing code
When cherry-picking feature work onto a squashed main branch with unrelated histories, use git cherry-pick range rather than git merge --allow-unrelated-histories to avoid massive conflicts
taplo fmt reorders TOML keys alphabetically — side-effect changes to unrelated crates' Cargo.toml files appear in git diff
For OTP-bearing edit modals, always echo back existing otp_self_approval and otp_cooldown_secs from the fetched config to prevent silent reset on save
Integration tests for network-dependent code need the broadcast receiver created before connect() — otherwise the EOSE and early events are missed and tests time out

friction: nostr-sdk v0.44 nip04 and nip44 features are not in default — caused compilation failures that required workspace Cargo.toml updates
nostr_sdk::prelude::* glob imports both nip04 and nip44 with wildcard re-exports causing 'unnecessary qualification' clippy errors for nostr_sdk::nips::nip04::decrypt

DM round-trip integration test initially timed out because broadcast::Receiver was created after connect() — required significant debugging (adding debug logging, checking notification variants) before identifying the root cause
git merge of helix-epoch onto squashed main produced 70+ conflicts from unrelated histories — had to abort and cherry-pick instead
biome JS formatter required a specific multi-line chaining style that wasn't applied by the initial write, causing CI failure
open_items: NIP-17 gift-wrapped DMs (requires complex event handling, less widely supported — deferred to future)
Metrics feature: 9 metric constants defined but no Prometheus histogram buckets or alerting rules configured yet
NOSTR_TEST_BOT_KEY and NOSTR_TEST_SENDER_KEY need to be added as GitHub Actions secrets for CI integration test job
Integration test CI workflow entry (provider-integration.yml equivalent for Nostr) not yet created
Matrix channel still routes through #[serde(flatten)] extra map rather than a named field — inconsistent with other channels but intentionally not changed to avoid scope creep
